claim_tested: Conformal quantiles calibrated on Marson T1D CD4+ Perturb-seq, applied UNCHANGED to an independent Perturb-seq dataset, still deliver near-nominal 80/90/95% coverage.

external_dataset.name: Norman et al. 2019 genome-scale genetic-interaction Perturb-seq
external_dataset.why_independent: Different lab, different cell type (K562 leukemia vs primary CD4+ T-cells), different perturbation panel, different sequencing run. The T1D pipeline never saw it. TRUE cross-dataset external validation.
external_dataset.raw_matrix: 33694 genes x 111668 cells, 362,199,631 nonzeros (streamed, not loaded)

frozen_calibration_provenance: q reconstructed from a global HGBR fit on all Marson pairs; widths 2q = 0.343/0.460/0.606 match committed conformal_lodo_receipt.json (0.342/0.458/0.603).

method_faithfulness.target: y = log1p( L2norm( DE zscore over shared readout panel, on-target gene excluded ) ) — IDENTICAL definition to Marson conformal.py; DE zscore = Welch two-sample stat (perturbation vs 11855 NegCtrl_NegCtrl cells) on log1p-CP10K expression.
method_faithfulness.condition_mapping: K562 has no TCR-stimulation timepoints; pre-registered primary = 'Rest' (unstimulated, closest biological analog).
method_faithfulness.base_predictor_note: Frozen base predictor is near-constant (pred sd=0.017 on Marson), so coverage depends on the residual DISTRIBUTION, not on feature-reconstruction details.

within_dataset_reference.description: FALLBACK reference (weaker): global frozen Marson q applied to held-out donor (same assay). Isolates cross-donor from cross-dataset shift.

diagnostic_offset_vs_scale.interpretation: Even after oracle mean-recentering, coverage stays well below nominal (0.41/0.49/0.68) because the Norman residual spread is 2.6x wider than the Marson calibration spread. Failure is BOTH location and scale — not a simple recalibratable offset.

honest_verdict: HONEST NEGATIVE. Frozen Marson T1D conformal quantiles do NOT transfer to an independent K562 Perturb-seq dataset: nominal 80/90/95% coverage collapses to 4.8/7.6/17.1%. The conformal guarantee is exchangeability-bound to the calibration distribution — it holds within-assay across held-out donors (79/90/95, matching the committed receipt) but NOT across a biologically distinct dataset. This is the expected behavior of split-conformal under distribution shift and is reported as a negative, not tuned away. The trust layer's coverage claim is valid ONLY for CD4+ T-cell Perturb-seq like its calibration data; it must be recalibrated per assay before use elsewhere.
